title: A Gapped Boundary of a Chiral Theory in 2+1d
speaker: Jeongwan Haah
abstract:
Bloomberg Lecture Hall
It is believed that topologically ordered gapped local Hamiltonians in 2+1d consisting of commuting terms may not realize a chiral anyon theory. There is even a formula that expresses the chiral central charge of the edge theory as an integral of commutators of the bulk Hamiltonian terms. Here, we follow an orthodox approach to quantum many-body physics to point out a strong but hidden role of tensor product algebras in this belief. We will give a locally generated, locally finite dimensional operator algebra on 2d and a local commuting Hamiltonian in that algebra, realizing the three-fermion anyon theory, which contradicts the belief. Furthermore, I will explain that the boundary of this three-fermion theory can be gapped out (but is not a boson condensate), again by some choice of a local operator algebra.
tags: IAS Joint SNS/SOM High Energy Theory Seminar

proof_stages.label: Observable-algebra setup for gapped systems
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by arguing that gappedness must be formulated relative to a chosen local observable algebra, then sets up lattice nets of finite-dimensional algebras, locality/commutation with a length parameter, and formal local Hamiltonians.

proof_stages.label: Haag duality as a locality diagnostic
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines a lattice version of Haag duality via commutants and complements, allowing bounded leakage, and uses simple examples and nonexamples to show what this property detects.

proof_stages.label: Invertible subalgebras and bounded-spread factorization
proof_stages.summary: A subalgebra A of a full tensor-product algebra is called invertible when, with B its commutant, the multiplication map A⊗B to the full algebra is an isomorphism whose inverse has bounded spread, and such invertibility implies Haag duality.

proof_stages.label: Commuting Hamiltonian realizing three-fermion order
proof_stages.summary: Inside the invertible algebra the speaker constructs a commuting local Hamiltonian with trivial local spectrum, no local redundancies, string operators creating pairs of excitations, and exactly the three nontrivial fermionic topological sectors, despite the usual chiral-central-charge obstruction expectations.

proof_stages.label: Half-plane restriction and gapped boundary
proof_stages.summary: A boundary system is defined by retaining generators whose reference points lie in a half-space, keeping bulk terms, adding special boundary terms, and proving the boundary is fully gapped because every operator commuting with all Hamiltonian terms is generated by those terms.

proof_stages.label: Failure of tensor-factor no-go arguments
proof_stages.summary: The speaker analyzes continuum and Levin-style arguments forbidding such a gapped boundary and identifies the failure point as the use of tensor-factor Hilbert-space complements or reduced density matrices, which the constructed algebra lacks.

proof_stages.label: Higher-dimensional QCA origin
proof_stages.summary: The exotic invertible subalgebra is explained as arising systematically from a three-dimensional locality-preserving automorphism/QCA, obtained by decomposing each two-dimensional layer into A and its commutant B and shifting the B component to the next layer.

proof_stages.label: Scope, variants, and open questions
proof_stages.summary: The closing Q&A discusses how far the construction might extend, its relation to stacking/copies, torus behavior, the one-dimensional triviality theorem for invertible subalgebras, and which chiral central charges or fusion rules might admit analogous constructions.

examples.name: decoupled extra degrees of freedom
examples.note: A gapped qubit system with extra uncoupled spins, and later two rows/chains with Hamiltonian on only one part, illustrates that the algebra of observables must be specified before asking whether a system or boundary is gapped.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker tests the added decoupled spins for gaplessness and separates them from the genuinely gapped subsystem
